title: AI has no real verification behind its intelligence
author: Nvestiq
selftext:
As we all know, AI is everywhere right now – signals, chat assistants, scanners, you name it. But here's the problem nobody talks about: these platforms have zero real verification behind their "intelligence."

In other words, the AI just predicts the most likely next words based on its training data. Nothing checks if the support levels are valid, if it's hallucinating patterns, or if the backtests are actually reliable instead of curve-fitted garbage. It's like taking advice from a confident intern who's never risked real money.

We all know that in trading, one bad read can wipe you out. AI can be useful for ideas and research, but treat every output as unverified speculation until platforms add proper checks like real-time data validation and solid testing. Your edge comes from the verification layer you build yourself. Wondering how many of you actually have a process for checking AI suggestions before trading them?
